description:
You are a FinOps analyst who maintains the small, command-line-only “Acme Cost Optimizer” utility.  
Your goal is to publish the next MINOR release.  
Follow these steps from a regular, non-root shell. (No sudo necessary.)

Current project layout:
  /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/
      ├── version.txt          (contains the single line “2.5.4” – nothing else)
      ├── CHANGELOG.md         (see format rules below)
      └── src/ …
      
Actions you must accomplish (≈3-4 shell commands total; feel free to inspect files first):

1. Read the current SemVer string in /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/version.txt (presently 2.5.4) and bump it to the next MINOR version (2.6.0).  
   • Write the new version string *alone on its own line* in exactly that file (no extra spaces or newline afterwards).

2. Create a new top-of-file entry in /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/CHANGELOG.md that follows **this exact template** (use today’s UTC date):
   ```
   ## [NEW_VERSION] - YYYY-MM-DD
   ### Added
   - FinOps: <one concise bullet (max 120 chars) describing the improvement you imagine>.
   ```
   Replace NEW_VERSION with 2.6.0 and the date with today’s date.  
   The “Added” section must be followed by a single dash bullet, then a blank trailing newline.  
   Do **not** alter older entries already present below.

3. To allow automated grading, create /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/release.log containing only these two lines (no leading/trailing spaces):
   ```
   version=2.6.0
   changelog_entry_added=true
   ```

Important formatting checks the grader will perform:
• /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/version.txt must equal exactly “2.6.0” (with a single trailing newline, no extra blank lines).  
• The top two non-blank lines of /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/CHANGELOG.md must match the regex:  
  `^## \[2\.6\.0\] - \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$` followed by `^### Added$`.  
• /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/release.log must contain exactly the two key/value lines shown above.

Do not modify any other files. When finished, you should have executed only a handful of basic shell commands such as cat, echo, printf or sed.
truth:
Initial state prior to agent interaction:
1. Directory /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/ exists.
2. /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/version.txt content (with single trailing newline):
   2.5.4\n
3. /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/CHANGELOG.md content:
   ## [2.5.4] - 2024-03-28
   ### Fixed
   - Corrected amortised reservation cost maths.

   ## [2.5.3] - 2024-03-10
   ### Added
   - Introduced forecast accuracy metric.

   (file ends with newline)

Expected final state after correct task completion:

A. /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/version.txt contains exactly:
   2.6.0\n

B. /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/CHANGELOG.md begins with the four lines (example using 2024-05-05; grader will use current date):
   ## [2.6.0] - 2024-05-05
   ### Added
   - FinOps: Optimized cost allocation query for multi-cloud aggregated reporting.

   followed by a blank line and then the previous contents starting with “## [2.5.4] - 2024-03-28”.

C. /home/user/acme-cost-optimizer/release.log exists with 0644 permissions and content:
   version=2.6.0\n
   changelog_entry_added=true\n
name: task_000000_e175f271
